title: Ionic conductivity of PPO-sulfonamide salt hybrids and their network polymers
abstract:
Ionic Conductivity of PPO-sulfonamide
Salt Hybrids and their Network Polymers
Yoichi Tominaga,Tomonobu Mizumo and Hiroyuki Ohno*
Tokyo University of Agriculture & Technology, Koganei, Tokyo 184-8588, Japan
ABSTRACT

New type of polymer electrolytes containing poly(pro-
pylene oxide) (PPO)/sulfonamide (SA) lithium salt (-
NLi-SO2-) unit were synthesized and the electrochemical
properties were analyzed. The mono- or di-charged
hybrids became amorphous salt and showed low glass
transition temperature, but the bulk ionic conductivity
was lower than 10ÿ6 S/cm at room temperature. The
introduction of terminal group having strong electron-
withdrawing effect on the SA end improved the ionic
conductivity. The maximum ionic conductivity was
observed (1.7  10ÿ5 S/cm at 30°C) when the terminal
group of PPO500-SA Li salt hybrid was trifluorometyl (-
CF3) group. On the other hand, two types of hybrid-
networks were also synthesized. They were rubbery solids
with amorphous phase. The ionic conductivity was less
than 10ÿ8 S/cm because of the relatively higher Tg. The
ionic conductivity of the network was improved more
than 1000 times by the addition of PPO500-SALi-CF3.
The Blend of the network and oligomer gave homo-
geneously mixed dry polymer electrolyte without phase
separation.
The
electrochemical
characteristics
was
analyzed with Li k PPO electrolyte k Li cell under dc
electric field. Copyright 2000 John Wiley & Sons, Ltd.
KEYWORDS: Poly(propylene oxide)/salt hybrid; Ionic
conductivity; Sulfonamide; Amorphous; Network poly-
mer
INTRODUCTION

Many kind of studies about solid polymer electro-
lytes based on some polyethers have hitherto been
reported since the ion conduction in poly(ethylene
oxide) (PEO)/sodium salt complex was firstly
observed [1]. Ionic conductivity of PEO-based
electrolyte is less than 10ÿ7 S/cm at room tempera-
ture because of the crystallization of PEO, whereas
the ionic conductivity at higher temperature is
more than 10ÿ4 S/cm. This means that the fast ion
conduction occurred only in the amorphous phase
[2]. It is well known that poly(propylene oxide)
(PPO) is a useful base polymer for ion conduction
similarly to PEO [3]. PPO is non-crystalline atactic
polymer and has low glass transition temperature
(Tg). Ionic conductivity of low molecular weight
(Mw) PPO-LiClO4 complex is more than 10ÿ6 S/cm
at
room
temperature
[4].
The
ion-conducting
mechanism has been analyzed by the fitting both
of VTF (1) and WLF (2) equation [5], the dielectric
response [6], or the VTF-viscosity parameters [7].

and B are constants. T0 is the temperature at which
the configurational entropy in the system reaches
zero, normally related to Tg. C1 and C2 are
constants.

 However,
the
large
decrease
of
the
ionic
conductivity for PPO-salt mixture is observed
when the PPO Mw increases [8, 9]. Moreover, the
microphase separation of PPO-salt complex is
observed when the salt concentration was low
[10]. To improve the ionic conductivity, introduc-
tion of highly dissociative salts in PPO without
phase separation is strongly required.
On the other hand, ion conductive properties of
PEO oligomers having salt part on the chain ends
(polyether/salt hybrid) have already been studied
in our previous works [11±13]. PEO/salt hybrids
become amorphous salt by the control of PEO Mw
in between 300 to 1000. These hybrids also enable to
keep the salt concentration high without phase
separation. The low Mw hybrid is in a similar
category to polymer-in-salt systems reported by
Angell et al. [14]. Particularly, the bulk ionic
conductivity is more than 10ÿ5 S/cm at room
temperature and the Tg is lower than ÿ60°C when
the sulfonamide (SA) salt was introduced on PEO
chain end [13]. This strategy should also work for
PPO. In this study, we tried to prepare a variety of
new hybrid systems ªPPO/SA salt hybridº for
higher ionic conductivity. Moreover, the electro-
chemical stability of Li k PPO electrolyte k Li cell
was also analyzed.

chloride (Bz-(SO2Cl)2) and naphthalene-1,3,6-tri-

salt

(24.63 g,
2.07  10ÿ1 mol) was added dropwise in the
mixture of Nph-(SO3Na)3 (10.0 g, 2.30  10ÿ2 mol)
and N,N-dimethylformamide (0.50 g, 6.90  10ÿ3
mol) under dry nitrogen gas, and the solution was
allowed to react at 90°C for 12 h. After the reaction,
the mixture was washed by chloroform and NaCl
was removed by filtration. The filtrate was evapo-
rated and the light brown powder (Nph-(SO2Cl)3)
was dried in vacuo at 30°C for 48 h.
Preparation of PPO/SA Li salt hybrids
PPO/SA salt hybrid-oligomer was synthesized by
the general method based on a previous work [12].
The structure of mono-charged (PPO500-SALi-R;
R = -CF3, -CH3, and -C6H5) and di-charged hybrid
(PPOm-(SALi-R)2;

2000,

material shifted from 2.48 to 2.061.90 ppm de-
pends on sulfonamide group (-NH-SO2R). The
proton signals for methyne and methylene group

adjacent to the amino group (-CH (-CH3)CH2NH2)
shifted from 3.103.25 to 3.413.44 ppm. The
proton signals for methane (CH3-SO2Cl, 3.70 ppm)
and benzene sulfonyl chloride (C6H5-SO2Cl, 7.55,
7.66, and 7.80 ppm) disappeared and shifted to 2.99
(-NH-SO2CH3), 7.347.36, and 7.897.91 ppm (-
NH-SO2C6H5), respectively. There was no by-
product (triethylammonium chloride) remained in
The hybrid-network was synthesized by the
interfacial polycondensation [15]. The synthesis
method for two types of PPO/SA Li salt hybrid-
networks is summarized in Scheme 2. Network-I
and II were synthesized from PEO2000-(NH2)2
(1.0 g, 5.0  10ÿ4 mol) and Nph-(SO2Cl)3 (0.14 g,
3.3  10ÿ4 mol), G-(PPO1000-NH2)3 (1.0 g, 3.3  10ÿ4
mol) and Bz-(SO2Cl)2 (0.14 g, 5.0  10ÿ4 mol),
respectively. The aqueous solution (0.2 ml) of LiOH
(0.02 g, 1.0  10ÿ3 mol) and the chloroform solution
(4.0 ml) of amines and sulfonyl chlorides were
mixed at 50°C for 24 h. After these cross-linking
reactions, insoluble rubbery solids in any kind of
solvents were obtained and washed by excess
methanol. Cation exchange reaction was carried
out in methanol solution with excess LiOH con-
centration. These polymers were dried in vacuo at
Thermal analysis was carried out with a differential
scanning calorimeter, SEIKO Instruments Inc. DSC-
5200. The temperature was swept from ÿ120 to
200°C with the heating rate of 10°C/min.
The ionic conductivity was measured by the
complex impedance method with Solartron-1260
impedance/gain-phase analyzer (Schlumberger) in
the frequency range from 10 to 1M Hz. The
impedance data was collected by the custom-
designed apparatus to depict the Arrhenius plots
of the ionic conductivity [16]. The temperature was
swept from 60 to 10°C with the cooling rate of
3.0°C/min. Sample cell was composed by the pair
of parallel stainless steel (St) electrodes with
0.50 mm-thick polypropylene (PP) spacer. The
sample cell preparation and the measurement
was carried out in a dry nitrogen gas-filled glove
The dc polarization current was measured by
potentiogalvanostat NPGS-2501 (Nikko Keisoku)
with 100 m-thick metallic lithium (Li) electrode
[17]. The cell was composed by the pair of Li films
fixed on St electrodes with PP spacer. These
operations were carried out in an argon gas-filled
glove box while the gas was circulated by the air
pump through the St pipe filled with molecular

Since all these hybrids showed no thermal re-
sponses over the Tg detected by DSC measurement,
these were confirmed to be amorphous salt. Mono-
charged lithium salt hybrids (PPO500-SALi-R) were
FIGURE 1. Temperature dependence of the ionic
conductivity for mono-charged (PPO Mw; m = 500,
R = ÿCF3(*), ÿC6H5(&), ÿCH3(~)) and di-charged
(m = 300(&), 2000(◊), 4000(!), R = ÿCH3) PPO/SA Li
FIGURE 2. Temperature dependence of the ionic
conductivity for the hybrid-network I (*), II (~), and the
blends of PPO500-SALi-CF3 (50 wt%) and I (*) or II (~).

Polym. Adv. Technol. 11, 524±528 (2000)
obtained as liquid electrolytes and the Tg was
lower than ÿ60°C (R = ÿCF3; ÿ65.6°C, ÿCH3;
ÿ67.7°C, ÿC6H5; ÿ61.1°C). The Tg of di-charged
hybrids (PPOm-SALi-CH3) increased with decreas-
ing the Mw of PPO part (i.e., m = 4000; ÿ64.9°C,
2000; ÿ60.5°C, 300; ÿ31.2°C) because of high Li ion
concentration to oxypropylene unit ([Li]/[OP]).
Temperature dependence of the ionic conductivity
for PPO/SA Li salt hybrid-oligomers is shown in
Figure 1. The ionic conductivity of PPOm-SALi-CH3
was less than 10ÿ7 S/cm at room temperature. The
value was almost the same as that for other hybrids
in spite of the different Mw of PPO. The hybrid
with Mw of 300 showed the highest Tg and had
large activation energy because of high Li ion
concentration ([Li]/[OP] = 38.7 mol%). On the other
hand, the ionic conductivity of PPO500-SALi-CH3
was over 10 times higher than that of PPOm-(SALi-
CH3)2 because of relatively low Tg. The difference
was caused by the existence of free PPO chains
having high segmental motion. Particularly, the
hybrid having trifluoromethyl group on the chain
end (PPO500-SALi-CF3) showed the highest ionic
conductivity of all these hybrids, i.e., more than
10ÿ5 S/cm at room temperature. Introduction of the
terminal groups having strong electron-withdraw-
ing effect largely enabled to improve the dissocia-
tion degree of sulfonamide group [12]. The ionic
conductivity was slightly lower than that of mono-
charged type PEO/SA salt hybrid having methox-
2.4  10ÿ5 S/cm) [13] and it was almost the same
value as that of PPO700-LiTFSI simple mixture
([Li]/[OP] = 8.5 mol%). Therefore, PPO oligomer is
concluded to be also effective polymer for high
ionic conductivity in the hybrid systems.
Hybrid-oligomers had low Tg and enabled to
show high ionic conductivity, however, it is
difficult to make self-standing film because these
were viscous liquid and have poor mechanical
properties. For the preparation of hybrid system in
the solid state, it is necessary to design the macro-
molecules such as network polymer. It has hitherto
been studied about the ion conductive behavior of
the PPO network [18±21] or branched polymer
complex having short PPO side chains [22, 23].
These PPO matrixes became rubber- or film-like
solid with low Tg and enabled to show high ionic
conductivity more than 10ÿ6 S/cm at room tem-
perature. The high ionic conductivity is caused by
the addition of the high dissociation salt such as
LiTFSI in PPO network [20] or the introduction of
free PPO segment [22]. Temperature dependence of
the ionic conductivity for two types of hybrid-
networks is shown in Figure 2. These hybrid-
networks became elastic rubber with amorphous
phase keeping relatively low Tg (I; ÿ57.3°C, II;
ÿ55.4°C). However, the ionic conductivity was
considerably lower than that of hybrid-oligomer,
less than 10ÿ8 S/cm at room temperature. The
decrease of the ionic conductivity was caused by
the increase of Tg and depression of salt dissocia-
tion, attributable to the formation of cross-linking.
For high ionic conductivity in the solid state, it is
important to construct a fast ion-conducting phase
in the network. Low Mw hybrid-oligomer (PPO500-
SALi-CF3, 50 wt%) was added in these networks.
As the results, the ionic conductivity was improved
about 1000 times and more than 10ÿ6 S/cm was
observed at room temperature. The addition of
hybrid-oligomer was effective to keep the network
homogeneously dry without phase separation. The
Tg of these blends was slightly lower than that of
hybrid-network without oligomer, and the DSC
curves showed only one glass transition tempera-
ture. In the case of the blend consist with poly(-
SALi-CF3 (50 wt%), two glass transition tempera-
tures attributable to the phase separation were
clearly confirmed from the DSC measurement. The
network polymer was more effective to form a
uniform hybrid system in the solid state since low
Mw hybrid-oligomer is easily introduced in the
The simple mixture of PEO or PPO and Li salt
showed gradual decreases in current under dc
polarization [17, 19]. On the other hand, the hybrid
system was required to be single-ion conductive
matrix since anions are fixed on the polymer
backbone [11, 21]. Figure 3 shows the electroche-
mical responses of Li knetwork-I  PPO500-SALi-
CF3 (50 wt%)k Li cell under applied voltage of
10 mV at 42°C. The Li metal film was used for this
experiment as a non-blocking electrode. As seen in
Figure 3(a), the impedance plots showed a large arc
due to the bulk resistance (Rb) and a small arc due
to the charge transfer resistance (Re) of the interface
between electrolyte and electrode. The Re was
relatively small compared to the Rb and the large
change was not observed even after 15 h. This
means that no reaction such as the production of a
passivation layer occurs at the interface. Figure 3(b)
shows the direct current stability of the Li knet-
work-I  PPO500-SALi-CF3k Li cell at 42°C for 15 h.
FIGURE 3. (a): Complex impedance plot for the blend of
hybrid-network I and PPO500-SALi-CF3 (50 wt%) with Li
metal electrodes at 42°C (the frequency range was from
0.1 to 1M Hz). (b): Time dependence of dc polarization
current for the Li kelectrolytek Li cell at 42°C.

A small decrease in the initial current was probably
caused by the slightly polarized low Mw of PPO-
sulfonamide anions. Since the stable current (0.13
A) was observed even after 15 h., the sample was
confirmed to be stable film without phase separa-
tion and no chemical reaction occurred at Li

synthesized as a new type of ion conductive matrix.
The ionic conductivity of PPO500-SALi-CF3 was the
highest of all PPO/salt hybrid systems (1.7  10ÿ5
S/cm at 30°C) and was almost the same as that for
PEO/SA salt hybrid. It was due to low Tg and high
dissociation degree of the terminal SA group. On
the other hand, the ionic conductivity of the hybrid-
networks was less than 10ÿ8 S/cm at room
temperature in spite that these were rubbery solid
with amorphous phase and showed relatively low
Tg below ÿ50°C. The ionic conductivity was
improved more than 1000 times by the addition
of PPO500-SALi-CF3 in the network. The addition
was effective to keep the network homogeneously
